data.name: User
data.description: End-user who places product orders and makes payments on the e-commerce platform

threats.title: Identity Spoofing
threats.description: An attacker impersonates a legitimate user by stealing login credentials then performs unauthorized transactions under the victim's account.
threats.mitigation:
- Enforce Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for all users.
- Add CAPTCHA to login forms.
- Require strong password policies.
- Apply rate limiting to block brute-force attempts.

threats.title: Transaction Fraud
threats.description: A user denies having placed an order or made a payment, causing transaction disputes and potential financial losses for the merchant.
threats.mitigation:
- Implement logging for every transaction.
- Send transaction confirmations via email.

data.name: E-Commerce Application
data.description: Core e-commerce application that manages product orders, presents payment options, processes payment requests via the payment gateway, and returns transaction confirmations to users

threats.title: Purchase Tampering
threats.description: An attacker manipulates order data via HTTP request tampering before it reaches the payment gateway, resulting in paying less than the actual price.
threats.mitigation: Validate all prices and order details in the backend.

threats.title: Elevation of Privilege
threats.description: An attacker exploits application vulnerabilities to gain administrative access.
threats.mitigation:
Apply the principle of least privilege across all application components.
- Perform input validation.

threats.title: Denial of Service
threats.description: An attacker floods the e-commerce application with massive request volumes or sends resource-intensive requests.
threats.mitigation:
Implement rate limiting, load balancing, and auto-scaling.
- Use protection (e.g., Cloudflare, AWS Shield). Apply request throttling and appropriate connection timeouts to limit resource exhaustion.

threats.title: Information Disclosure melalui Error Handling
threats.description: The application exposes sensitive information (stack traces, database configurations, API keys) through improperly handled error messages, giving attackers reconnaissance data for further exploitation.
threats.mitigation: Implement custom error pages that reveal no technical details. Use centralized error handling middleware. Log detailed errors only server-side. Disable debug mode in production environments.

data.name: Payment Gateway
data.description: Third-party payment gateway that processes payment transactions, forwards payment authorization requests to banks/financial service providers, and returns transaction results to the e-commerce application

threats.title: Spoofing Payment Gateway
threats.description: An attacker creates a fake payment page via DNS hijacking or phishing to impersonate the legitimate payment gateway, stealing users' credit card numbers, CVVs, and e-wallet credentials.
threats.mitigation: Use TLS/SSL with certificate pinning. Verify the payment gateway's SSL certificate on every connection. Implement DNSSEC to prevent DNS-based redirection. Use payment tokenization so raw card data never passes through the merchant's infrastructure.

threats.title: Tampering pada Respons Payment Gateway
threats.description: An attacker performs a man-in-the-middle attack to modify the payment gateway's response, such as changing a 'failed' payment status to 'success', causing orders to be fulfilled without valid payment.
threats.mitigation: Apply HMAC-SHA256 digital signatures to all payment gateway responses. Verify signatures server-side in the e-commerce application. Use webhook callbacks with a shared secret key for asynchronous notifications. Perform regular reconciliation between e-commerce records and payment gateway data.

threats.title: Replay Attack pada Transaksi Pembayaran
threats.description: An attacker captures a valid payment request and retransmits (replays) it to create duplicate or unauthorized transactions.
threats.mitigation: Assign a unique idempotency key to every transaction. Include a nonce and timestamp in each request. Reject requests with expired timestamps. Store and validate idempotency keys server-side to prevent duplicate processing.

data.name: Bank / Penyedia Layanan Keuangan
data.description: Bank or financial service provider that validates and processes payment transactions (credit card, e-wallet, bank transfer)

threats.title: Spoofing Respons Bank
threats.description: An attacker forges responses from the bank/financial service provider to approve transactions that should be declined, or to decline legitimate transactions.
threats.mitigation: Use mutual TLS (mTLS) between the payment gateway and the bank. Verify the bank's digital certificate on every connection. Implement message authentication codes (MAC) on all communications. Conduct daily reconciliation between payment gateway records and bank statements.

data.name: Database Pesanan & Transaksi
data.description: Database storing order data, transaction statuses, payment details, and audit logs

threats.title: Tampering pada Data Database
threats.description: An attacker who gains database access (via SQL injection or compromised credentials) modifies order data, alters payment statuses, or deletes transaction records.
threats.mitigation: Encrypt data at rest. Use parameterized queries for all database access. Enforce strict database access controls following the least privilege principle. Enable database audit logging. Deploy a Database Activity Monitoring (DAM) solution.

threats.title: Information Disclosure - Kebocoran Data Pembayaran
threats.description: Sensitive user payment data (credit card numbers, bank details) stored in the database is leaked through unauthorized access, unencrypted backups, or SQL injection.
threats.mitigation: Never store raw credit card data; use tokenization from the payment gateway instead. Encrypt all sensitive data at rest with AES-256. Enforce PCI DSS compliance. Encrypt all database backups. Restrict database access exclusively to the application server layer.

threats.title: Denial of Service pada Database
threats.description: An attacker causes database overload through resource-intensive queries or excessive database connections, preventing payment transactions from being processed.
threats.mitigation: Implement connection pooling and query timeouts. Use database replication with automatic failover. Apply query optimization and proper indexing. Monitor database performance and configure alerts for anomalous behavior.

data.name: Log Audit Transaksi
data.description: Audit log storage for all payment transaction activities, used for non-repudiation and forensic investigation

threats.title: Tampering pada Audit Log
threats.description: An attacker who gains system access modifies or deletes audit logs to cover traces of malicious activity, hindering forensic investigation.
threats.mitigation: Store logs in append-only (immutable) storage. Use a centralized log management system (SIEM). Apply digital signatures to each log entry. Stream logs in real-time to a separate external storage system. Enforce strict access controls on log storage with no delete permissions.

data.name: Konfirmasi Pembayaran & Status Pesanan
data.description: E-commerce application sends payment confirmation results and updates the order status back to the user

threats.title: Spoofing Konfirmasi Pembayaran
threats.description: An attacker sends a fake payment confirmation to the user (via phishing email or fraudulent notification) to deceive them into believing a transaction succeeded or failed.
threats.mitigation: Send confirmations only through verified official channels (email with DKIM/SPF/DMARC, in-app notifications). Include unique transaction details known only to the user and the system. Educate users to verify order status directly within the application.

data.name: Pemesanan Produk & Detail Pembayaran
data.description: User submits product order data and enters payment details (payment method, card/e-wallet/bank information) to the e-commerce application

threats.title: Penyadapan Data Pembayaran (Eavesdropping)
threats.description: An attacker intercepts communication between the user's browser and the e-commerce server via a man-in-the-middle attack (especially on unsecured public WiFi networks) to steal payment data.
threats.mitigation: Enforce HTTPS with TLS 1.3. Implement HSTS (HTTP Strict Transport Security) headers. Use certificate pinning on mobile applications. Educate users to avoid making transactions on unsecured public networks.

threats.title: Tampering pada Request Pembayaran
threats.description: An attacker modifies payment data sent from the user to the e-commerce server (e.g., changing the payment amount or redirecting the destination account) using a proxy interceptor tool.
threats.mitigation: Validate all payment data server-side. Apply HMAC-based integrity checks on payment requests. Use client-side anti-tampering mechanisms. Enforce Content Security Policy (CSP) headers to prevent unauthorized script injection.

data.name: Query & Update Pesanan/Transaksi
data.description: E-Commerce Application reads and updates order data and transaction statuses in the database

threats.title: SQL Injection pada Query Database
threats.description: An attacker injects malicious SQL commands through unvalidated input fields to read, modify, or delete order and transaction data in the database.
threats.mitigation: Use parameterized queries/prepared statements for all database access. Apply strict input validation and sanitization. Use an ORM (Object-Relational Mapping) layer. Restrict database user privileges to only the minimum required operations.

data.name: Hasil Transaksi
data.description: Payment Gateway sends transaction results (success/failure, transaction ID, details) back to the e-commerce application

threats.title: Spoofing Webhook Callback
threats.description: An attacker sends a forged webhook callback to the e-commerce endpoint, faking a successful payment notification from the payment gateway to obtain goods without paying.
threats.mitigation: Verify the digital signature on every webhook callback using a shared secret key. Validate the sender's IP address against the payment gateway's IP whitelist. Re-verify the transaction status by calling the payment gateway's API directly after receiving any webhook.

data.name: Permintaan Pembayaran
data.description: E-commerce application sends a payment processing request to the Payment Gateway via API, including amount, currency, payment method, and token

threats.title: Tampering pada API Request ke Payment Gateway
threats.description: An attacker who has compromised the e-commerce server or is performing a MITM attack on internal communications modifies payment requests sent to the payment gateway (altering the amount or destination account).
threats.mitigation: Use API keys and secrets with regular rotation schedules. Implement request signing with HMAC-SHA256. Use mutual TLS (mTLS) between the e-commerce application and the payment gateway. Validate request integrity on both ends.

data.name: Write Audit Log
data.description: Payment Gateway writes an audit log entry for every processed transaction

data.name: Respons Validasi Pembayaran
data.description: Bank/financial service provider sends a payment validation response (approved/declined, authorization code) back to the Payment Gateway

data.name: Permintaan Validasi Pembayaran
data.description: Payment Gateway sends a payment validation and authorization request to the Bank/financial service provider

threats.title: Information Disclosure pada Jaringan Pembayaran
threats.description: Sensitive transaction data transmitted between the payment gateway and the bank is exposed due to improperly configured encryption or vulnerabilities in the communication protocol.
threats.mitigation: Enforce end-to-end encryption on all communications. Maintain PCI DSS compliance across the entire data path. Use a dedicated secure network (not the public internet) for payment gateway-to-bank communication where possible. Deploy Hardware Security Modules (HSM) for cryptographic key management.

data.name: Payment Gateway / Banking Network
data.description: Trust boundary between the payment gateway and the banking network

data.name: Internet / Public Network
data.description: Trust boundary between the user (public internet) and the e-commerce infrastructure

data.name: E-Commerce Internal / Payment Network
data.description: Trust boundary between the e-commerce infrastructure and the payment gateway/banking network
